parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-278 (RP08.P02.M06.S15-RP08.P02.M07.S02, risk class A, 10 units, descriptor-only email-dms core service fixture tail pack). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly held false: manifest.json status is implemented_pending_review with production_ready false, pack_level_claude_review pending, and valid_review_receipts empty, so no production-readiness or enterprise-trust claim is asserted ahead of human final approval. Cross-file consistency verified across registry.js binding, service.js, validators.js, contracts/email-dms-core-contract.json (current_pack_id CP00-278), docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json, manifest.json, review-baseline.json, README.md, the validator script, and test/model.test.js: pack id, range, upstream/next chain (CP00-277 -> CP00-278 -> CP00-279), deliverable distribution {ui:1,implementation:2,failure_recovery:2,test:4,contract:1}, micro_title distribution {Synthetic Fixture Set:8,Test And Golden Case Set:2}, and the 10 required section rows all agree. Descriptor-only/synthetic-only/no-real-data and the full no-runtime/no-leak flag set are present; attestation carries promotes_claude_to_final_approval false and claims_enterprise_trust false. Authority boundary intact: Claude remains read_only_independent_reviewer and not final approver, and Hermes control-plane patterns appear only in deterministic review-packet/receipt builders, not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false). CP178+ review-evidence hardening clears: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, non-zero 1246-byte stdout, well-formed JSON result), with no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output, or missing required fields. command-evidence.json shows ~20 commands all exit 0 (node --check x5, node --test, npm test, npm run validate/build, closeout-pack/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validators, git diff --check), no_real_data true, writes_product_state false, Hermes gate H08 passed. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected outputs of this review step and their current absence is not a defect. No P0, P1, P2, or P3 discrepancies identified.
